401.description: Returned when the user is not authorized.

404.description: Returned when the resource does not exist.

post.description:
Extracts named entities from a phrase. If no entities can be found, it returns an empty collection of entities. Each entity contains one or more (ambiguous) interpretations. Each recognized entity will most likely contain only one possible interpretation. 

You can limit the named entities to search for by explicitly providing the entity types. If no types are specified upfront, all entity types will be extracted and returned if possible. 

If no language is specified upfront, the Named Entity Recognition API will try to infer it automatically before proceeding with named entity recognition. An inferred or pre-specified language that is not supported will yield an error.
post.summary: Extract named entities from a phrase

401.description: Returned when the user is not authorized.

404.description: Returned when the resource does not exist.

get.description: Lists the supported entity types per language.

v1AmountOfMoneyValue.description: An amount of money expressed in whole units, cents and a currency.

cents.description: The number of cents. If the entity is `$3.05`, then `cents` is `5`.
cents.title: Cents

currencyName.description: The name of the currency. Some currency names are not ISO4217 compliant. For those cases, this will hold the currency as a string. These include `"Cent"`, `"Dinar"`, `"Dollar"`, `"Rial"`, `"Riyal"`, `"Pound"`, `"PTS"`.
currencyName.title: Currency Name

units.description: The whole units of money. If the entity is `$3.05`, then `units` is `3`.
units.title: Units

v1AmountOfMoneyValue.title: AmountOfMoneyValue

v1CurrencyCodeIso4217Value.description: A wrapper for the currency code enum.
v1CurrencyCodeIso4217Value.title: CurrencyCodeIso4217Value

v1DefaultValues.description: A normalized value of an entity, e.g. for time the ISO8601 date format.

value.description: The normalized value of an entity, e.g. for time the ISO8601 date format.
value.title: Value

v1DefaultValues.title: DefaultValues

v1DurationValues.description: A duration and the unit of the duration. Durations are normalized to whole seconds.

unit.description: The unit used to indicate the duration.
unit.title: Unit

value.description: The part of the phrase that expresses a duration.
value.title: Value

v1DurationValues.title: DurationValues

v1Entity.description: A (named) entity. Entities are sub-phrases with structured information, such as person name, locations, time, quantities etc. The process of extracting these entities from natural language is called Named Entity Recognition (NER).

ambiguous.description: Describes if the parsed value has multiple possible values. For example `tomorrow at 7` could either mean `tomorrow 07:00` or `tomorrow 19:00`.
ambiguous.title: Ambiguous

endCharIndex.description: The ending character index of the entity in the given phrase.
endCharIndex.title: End Char Index

entityValues.description: The collection of entity values. If the entity is ambiguous (see `ambiguous`), this collection will contain more than one value. For example `tomorrow at 7` could either mean `tomorrow 07:00` or `tomorrow 19:00`.
entityValues.title: Entity Values

startCharIndex.description: The starting character index of the entity in the given phrase.
startCharIndex.title: Start Char Index

text.description: The raw text from the phrase where the entity was found.
text.title: Text

type.description: The type of entity that was found. This entity type also determines which `values` are returned.
type.title: Type

v1Entity.title: Entity

valueComments: Entity type that describes amount of money.

Entity type that describes credit card number. Supported credit card types are [Visa](https//www.visa.com), [American Express (Amex)](https://www.americanexpress.com), [Discover](https://www.discover.com), [Mastercard](https://www.mastercard.com) and [DinerClub](https://www.dinersclub.com). Note: if a credit card number is [invalid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luhn_algorithm), it will not be extracted from the phrase.
Entity type that describes distance. Supports both the metric system (meters, kilometers etc.) and imperial (inch, yard etc.) measurements.
Entity type that describes duration. Durations are normalized to whole seconds.
Entity type that describes email address.
Entity type that describes a numeral. Can be written as digits, words, fractions, floating point numbers, as well as some language-specific ways of expressing certain numbers, such as `"a dozen"`.
Entity type that describes an ordinal. Can be written as digits or as words.
Entity type that describes a phone number. Note that this phone number is not checked for validity. Supports leading area codes too.
Entity type that describes a quantity of a product using a certain unit. Example use-cases are parsing of cooking recipes and grocery lists.
Entity type that describes a temperature. Supports values to be expressed as words, digits, fractions and floating point numbers. Units can be degree, Celsius or Fahrenheit.
Entity type that describes a time. Supported values are exact times, but also time expressed as words, such as `"tomorrow"` or holidays such as `"GroundHog Day"`.
Entity type that describes an URL. Will only be populated if the URL is formatted correctly.
Entity type that describes a volume. Supports both metric and imperial measurements.
Entity type that describes an interval.
Entity type that describes a person, including fictional person names.
Entity type that describes an organization, such as companies, agencies, institutions etc.
Entity type that describes a product. Vehicles and types of food etc.
Entity type that describes a place. Can be cities, countries, states, continents etc., but also descriptions of locations such as "East of the river".
Entity type that describes a country.

v1EntityValuesWrapper.description: A wrapper for entity values with varying type and structure.

creditCardNumber.description: A credit card number. Supported credit card types are [Visa](https//www.visa.com), [American Express (Amex)](https://www.americanexpress.com), [Discover](https://www.discover.com), [Mastercard](https://www.mastercard.com) and [DinerClub](https://www.dinersclub.com).
creditCardNumber.title: Credit Card Number

distance.description: A distance value. Supports both the metric system (meters, kilometers etc.) and imperial (inch, yard etc.) measurements.
distance.title: Distance

duration.description: The duration value. Durations are normalized to whole seconds.
duration.title: Duration

emailAddress.description: The email address value. Will not be populated if an email address is invalid (for example due to misspelling).
emailAddress.title: Email Address

interval.description: The interval value. Intervals are given by a start and an end time.
interval.title: Interval

numeral.description: The numeral value. Can be written as digits, words, fractions, floating point numbers, as well as some language-specific ways of expressing certain numbers, such as `"a dozen"`.
numeral.title: Numeral

ordinal.description: The ordinal value. Can be written as digits or as words.
ordinal.title: Ordinal

person.description: The person name, including fictional person names.
person.title: Person

phoneNumber.description: The phone number value. Note that this phone number is not checked for validity. Supports leading area codes too.
phoneNumber.title: Phone Number

product.description: The product value. Includes vehicles, devices, types of food etc.
product.title: Product

productQuantity.description: The quantity of a product using a certain unit. Example use-cases are parsing of cooking recipes and grocery lists.
productQuantity.title: Product Quantity

temperature.description: The temperature value. Supports values to be expressed as words, digits, fractions and floating point numbers. Units can be degree, Celsius or Fahrenheit.
temperature.title: Temperature

time.description: The time value. Supported values are exact times, but also time expressed as words, such as `"tomorrow"` or holidays such as `"GroundHog Day"`.
time.title: Time

url.description: The URL value. Will only be populated if the URL is formatted correctly.
url.title: Url

volume.description: The volume value. Supports both metric and imperial measurements.
volume.title: Volume

v1EntityValuesWrapper.title: EntityValuesWrapper

v1IntervalValues.description: A start time and an end time of an interval.

end.description: The normalized end date in ISO8601 date format.
end.title: End

start.description: The normalized start date in ISO8601 date format.
start.title: Start

v1LanguageCode.description: Language code in ISO639-3 format. More info can be found here: https://iso639-3.sil.org/code_tables/639/data.

v1ListSupportedLanguagesResponses.description: A response with a collection of languages and the entity types supported by those languages.

entitiesPerLanguage.description: Collection of supported entity types per language.
entitiesPerLanguage.title: Entities Per Language

v1ListSupportedLanguagesResponses.title: ListSupportedLanguagesResponses

v1PredictEntitiesRequest.description:
A request for predicting the entities from a given phrase. This process is also referred to as named entity recognition. It is possible to pre-specify the language when calling this endpoint. Otherwise, the Named Entity Recognition API will attempt to infer the language and proceed with extraction of the entities. 

If no entities could be found, it will return an empty collection.

example.phrase: Today we're in Amsterdam, building two chatbots using https://messagebird.com/en/solutions/faq-bots

entityTypes.description: A collection of entity types that can be detected. If empty, all found types are returned.
entityTypes.title: Entity Types

language.description: The language of the given phrase. If you're unsure of the language of a phrase, use our Language Detection API.
language.title: Language

phrase.description: The phrase to extract named entities from.
phrase.example: Today we're in Amsterdam, building two chatbots using https://messagebird.com/en/solutions/faq-bots
phrase.title: Phrase

timezone.description: The name of the timezone the samples should be detected in. For time or date related entities this ensures that the values are converted to the correct timezone. For an overview of available timezones see [this list](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones).
timezone.title: Timezone

v1PredictEntitiesRequest.title: PredictEntitiesRequest

v1PredictEntitiesResponse.description: Response payload that is returned when predicting entities. Contains an empty list when no entities could be found.

entities.description: The collection of entities that were extracted from the given phrase. The collection is empty if no entities could be found.
entities.title: Entities

v1PredictEntitiesResponse.title: PredictEntitiesResponse

v1ProductQuantityValue.description: A quantity of a product using a certain unit. Example use-cases are parsing of cooking recipes and shopping.
v1ProductQuantityValue.title: ProductQuantityValue

v1SupportedEntitiesForLanguage.description: A language and the entity types supported by that language.

entityTypes.description: Collection of the supported entity types for this language.
entityTypes.title: Entity Types

v1SupportedEntitiesForLanguage.title: SupportedEntitiesForLanguage

v1UnitValues.description: An amount and a unit of the amount.
v1UnitValues.title: UnitValues

tags.description:
The MessageBird Named Entity Recognition API extracts and parses a fixed set of entities from incoming phrases. A phrase is usually the text from an incoming message of your customer. This can happen in the context of receiving a Conversations API message or when receiving an SMS message, just to name a few. 

An entity contains structured information, such as person names, time, durations, locations etc. The process of finding and parsing entities from phrases is known as named entity recognition. 

Going from an unstructured phrase to structured data can be helpful for building intelligent conversational systems, such as chatbots. Chatbots often need to repeat information that was already said, for example to confirm whether they have extracted the right information. In other cases you might want your chat bot to call an internal or external API with a structured object. These things become straightforward using the MessageBird Named Entity Recognition API.
tags.name: NamedEntityRecognition

externalDocs.description: Find our official documentation in the MessageBird Docs
